summary: ai-governance may define MCP contracts and references, but it must not absorb MCP runtime execution.
central_allows: MCP manifests, registries, allowlists, and contract bundles.
central_forbids: Running MCP service runtimes inside ai-governance.
Owning personal knowledge state or long-lived MCP operational state.
Treating external MCP output as a substitute for human approval or release gates.
service_runtime_rule: MCP runtime adapters and real invocation surfaces stay in the owning service repository.
External MCPs may be used by governed services only through explicit allowlists.
Service-local evidence from MCP usage stays with the service unless separately normalized.

notes: obsidian-mcp may serve as a read-only context engine.
obsidian-mcp must remain outside ai-governance as an independent service.
Knowledge retrieval is advisory support, not a governance decision authority.
